The Florida legislature has a strong Republican supermajority and Governor Ron DeSantis has consistently championed strict election integrity laws. Evidence shows that a bill requiring proof of citizenship for first-time voter registrants (such as HB 1381 or similar omnibus election bills) was actively advancing late in the 2025 legislative session (e.g., passing committee around April 17, as well as being explicitly highlighted in an April 18 CNN article on red states' push for citizenship checks). While direct confirmation of the final floor vote at Sine Die (May 2) didn't surface in my queries, such high-priority GOP bills in Florida routinely pass. Given the momentum, the strong partisan alignment, and the fact that similar laws often pass under these conditions in FL, I estimate a 75% chance that the legislation was enacted by the cutoff or will be signed into law before July 1, 2025.

A blockade of a Taiwanese seaport by the PRC would represent a massive escalation, essentially an act of war, which has never happened in recent decades. As of the May 2025 knowledge cutoff, there are no reports of an actual blockade occurring. While China conducted military drills in April 2025 that simulated 'joint blockade and control,' the US categorized these as 'intimidation tactics' and 'destabilizing behavior' rather than an actual blockade. For the question to resolve Yes, China would not only need to transition from drills to an actual blockade within the two-month period before July 1, 2025, but the US government would also need to publicly accuse it of specifically 'blockading at least one of Taiwan's seaports.' Geopolitical forecasts generally estimate the probability of such an extreme escalation in any given short-term period to be near zero. Given the lack of current blockade actions and the short timeframe remaining, the likelihood of this occurring and being formally accused by the US by July 2025 is effectively at the minimum bound of 2%.
